_invariant: Real PAID Network (V1, exploited contract 0x8c86…8df) infinite-mint, 2021-03-05. ALL values RE-CAPTURED & VERIFIED on-chain (Alchemy ETH archive, recapture 2026-06-07): totalSupply @ 11979831/11979832/11979840 = 594717455710e18 / 535245710139e18 / 594717455710e18 ✓; mint Transfer(0x0→0x18738290…, 59471745571e18, tx 0x4bb1…55a0) @ 11979840 ✓; burn Transfer(0xd500aa2c…→0x0, 59471745571e18, tx 0x3a48…4b9b) @ 11979832 ✓; owner()=0x53bc…44cc ✓; decimals()=18 ✓; block ts 1614967279 (burn) & 1614967389 (mint) = 110s gap ✓. The attacker (compromised owner key 0x53bc…44cc) BURNED 59,471,745.571 PAID from 0xd500aa2c… (tx 0x3a48…4b9b, 18:01:19Z, block 11979832) then 110s later MINTED the SAME 59,471,745.571 PAID from 0x0 to NON-authorized exploiter 0x18738290… (tx 0x4bb1…55a0, 18:03:09Z, block 11979840). Net totalSupply over the window is FLAT (594,717,455.710 → 594,717,455.710), so a net-supply-diff monitor (d05) and an equal-amount mint/burn reconciliation (d11) are BOTH EVADED by the burn-then-remint. The single-tx %-of-supply mint check is the only thing that sees the individual 10%-of-supply unauthorized mint. snapshot_ts/now is set ~1h after so any window logic is past its settle. See snapshots/fidelity.json (90.9% measured; only off-chain price stays asserted).

metadata._note: MEASURED on-chain (recapture 2026-06-07): exploiter 0x18738290… is NOT the authorized owner/minter owner()=0x53bc…44cc (read @ 11979831), so the mint is unauthorized (non-home, non-allowlisted). The mint is 59,471,745.571 / 594,717,455.710 = 10.00% of supply = 1000 bps (>= single_tx_mint_bps). The equal 110s-earlier burn keeps net supply flat AND matches the mint within the 1800s recon window — the burn-remint EVASION. Only the single-tx %-of-supply check catches it. mint ts 1614967389, burn ts 1614967279 (gap 110s) both read from block headers.
